name: 高博德
current_positions: Vice-Chairman of the Institute of International Finance

work_experience.position: 27-year career: management trainee to CEO of Citibank in SE Asia, Australia and New Zealand; country officer in Malaysia and Singapore
Left Citigroup briefly to start a dot-com, then re-joined

person_relationships.description: Tan Su Shan was appointed deputy CEO in August 2024 and succeeded Gupta as CEO of DBS Group on March 28, 2025

political_stances.stance_content: Asia is no longer the factory of the world, it is the marketplace of the world. Anyone not looking at the Asian growth story over the next 10-15-20 years is missing a big opportunity.
Cautious about Web 3.0 implications - questioning whether mankind prefers to live in a world without organising principles such as countries, states, central banks, and regulators.
DBS needed to think like big tech - frame of reference should be Amazon or Alibaba, not other banks. Led digital transformation of DBS into one of the world's best digital banks.

major_achievements.achievement: Appointed CEO of DBS Group, transforming it from a local bank into a regional Asian powerhouse
DBS named one of the world's best banks by multiple international publications under his leadership
Led DBS to launch two new exchanges and undertake two acquisitions
Awarded the William Seidman Award for Lifetime Achievement in financial services
Over 15 years, grew DBS portfolio and transformed it into a global powerhouse for digital banking with cloud-native platforms and APIs

biography_summary: Piyush Gupta is a Singaporean banker of Indian origin who served as CEO of DBS Group from 2009 to March 2025. Born in Meerut, India, he started his career at Citibank in 1982, spending 27 years in various senior roles across Asia including country officer in Malaysia, Singapore, and Indonesia, and CEO of Citibank in SE Asia, Australia and New Zealand. He left Citibank briefly in 2000 to start a dot-com before rejoining. At DBS, he led the bank's digital transformation, thinking like big tech companies rather than traditional banks. Under his 15+ year tenure, DBS grew into one of the world's best digital banks. He was awarded the William Seidman Award for Lifetime Achievement. He is vice-chairman of the Institute of International Finance and a board member of Enterprise Singapore. A Singapore citizen since 2009 (with Overseas Citizenship of India), he is married to physiotherapist Ruchira Gupta and has two children.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with comprehensive career milestones (27 years at Citibank, 15+ years at DBS), family members, political stances on Asian economy/digital transformation/Web3, major achievements including William Seidman Award from Wikipedia and web search
